questions.question: What is Git?
questions.explanation: Git is used to track changes in files and manage versions.

questions.question: What command is used to initialize a Git repository?
What happens if the folder containing the Git repository gets deleted?
options: The entire project history is gone
questions.answer: The entire project history is gone
questions.explanation: This means that all commits, branches, and configurations are lost.

questions.question: What command is used to clear the screen?
questions.explanation: This command clears everything on the screen.

questions.question: How do you create a simple file in Git?
questions.explanation: This is the name of your file (app).

questions.question: What command is used to check the status?
questions.explanation: This shows whether Git can see the file app.txt.

questions.question: How do you stage a file in Git?
questions.explanation: This prepares the changes for the next commit.

questions.question: What command is used to make a commit in Git?
questions.explanation: This creates a snapshot of the project at this moment.

questions.question: What is a Commit in Git?
options: A snapshot of our project at this moment
The first version of our file
A way to track changes in files
A type of database management system
questions.answer: A snapshot of our project at this moment
questions.explanation: A commit is the first snapshot that is why it says initial commit.

questions.question: How do you view the history of commits?
questions.explanation: This shows the history of all commits.
